metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Why this cell exists

This is the early first-contact visibility check added by #599. When kiro-cli was onboarded, its model name and context utilization were invisible on the dashboard, but the gap only surfaced deep in section 5 (model-identification, 5.2) — far too late for a first-contact triage. This scenario (1.8) pulls the same visibility assertion forward to the very first trivial turn with a DEFAULT-launched agent, so any new column that can't surface model + context is caught immediately rather than passing the early rows and failing late. It is the agent-agnostic, default-launch sibling of 5.2 (which deliberately launches a NON-default model via --model).

## Reasoning

Claude Code records the active model on every assistant event under `message.model` (and on some system/metadata events under `model` / `request.model` / `metadata.model`). On a basic turn with the DEFAULT model, the first assistant event still carries `message.model`, so the parser captures the name with no `--model` flag needed. `extractClaudeCodeModel` (parser.go) walks raw.model / raw.request.model / raw.metadata.model / raw.message.model and runs the result through `tailer.NormalizeModelName`, producing a stable, non-empty string (e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-5`). Acceptance bullet `metrics.model_name != "unknown"` is therefore satisfied from the first assistant event onward.

Context utilization needs three inputs, all present on a single default-model turn:
1. ModelName — from extractClaudeCodeModel (above), non-empty after the first assistant event.
2. TotalTokens — extractClaudeCodeTokens pulls a usage block (raw.usage / raw.message.usage / raw.response.usage) via findUsageMap + tailer.ExtractUsage; every assistant turn reports usage, so TotalTokens > 0 after wait_turn.
3. ContextWindow — extractClaudeCodeModel sets it from raw.context_management.context_window when present (the most accurate source) or the [1m] suffix heuristic; otherwise ComputeContextUtilization falls back to the capacity manager's per-model window (capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(modelName).ContextWindow). For a known default model the alias/capacity table always supplies a positive window.

`ComputeContextUtilization` (tailer_metrics.go) early-returns (0, 0, unknown) only while TotalTokens==0 OR ModelName==""; once the first turn completes both are populated, so it returns ContextWindow > 0 and utilization = totalTokens/contextWindow*100 > 0 (non-zero because any turn consumes tokens). Acceptance bullets `context_window > 0` and `context_utilization_percentage > 0` both land after the first turn_done.

The state arc is the plain basic-turn arc: ready → working → ready, anchored to the same process-scan / transcript machinery exercised by 1.1 (session-start) and 5.2.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

Before the first assistant event, ModelName is "" and ContextWindow may read 0 — a zero-turn session carries no model/context assertion. The acceptance bullets are explicitly anchored to the first turn_done, after wait_turn, so this transient is out of scope. The recipe must complete at least one turn.
caveats: ContextWindow may briefly read 0 between session detection and the first assistant event (the same transient noted in 5.2). All acceptance assertions are checked only AFTER the first turn_done / wait_turn, not at presession_created — so the transient never violates the contract.

If the LiteLLM/capacity alias table lags behind a newly released default model name, ContextWindow falls back to the 32k sentinel with ContextWindowUnknown=true; ModelName is still correct and context_window is still > 0, so the acceptance bullets still pass — utilization is just computed against the sentinel window.

model_name is the agent's own normalized label; per the acceptance criteria a routing alias (e.g. "auto") counts when that is what the agent itself displays. Claude Code reports a concrete model id on the default turn, so this is not exercised here.

sources.note: extractClaudeCodeModel walks raw.model / raw.request.model / raw.metadata.model / raw.message.model, normalizes via NormalizeModelName, and sets ContextWindow from raw.context_management.context_window (or the [1m] suffix). No --model flag needed: message.model is present on the first default-model assistant event.
findUsageMap + extractClaudeCodeTokens pull the usage block (raw.usage / message.usage / response.usage) so TotalTokens > 0 after the first turn — the numerator for context utilization.

ComputeContextUtilization: early-returns (0,0,unknown) only while TotalTokens==0 or ModelName==""; once both are populated returns ContextWindow (override else capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity) and utilization = totalTokens/contextWindow*100 > 0. computeContextUtilization writes ContextWindow + ContextUtilization onto SessionMetrics.
Acceptance: metrics.model_name non-empty and != unknown after first turn_done; context_utilization_percentage > 0; context_window > 0; topology ready->working->ready; final ready.

The recorded+full sibling cell (non-default --model launch). Same parser/metrics path; strongest evidence that claudecode surfaces model + context. This 1.8 cell is the default-launch, first-contact variant.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or run-cell.sh isolated mode)
no other claudecode session uses the recipe's UUID
launch with the DEFAULT model — NO --model flag (this scenario asserts default-launch visibility, unlike 5.2)
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID (run-cell.sh does this via uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)
Launch claude with NO --model flag so the default model's name flows through message.model from turn 1
Assertions on model_name / context_window / context_utilization_percentage are checked AFTER turn_end — ContextWindow may briefly read 0 before the first assistant event (per assessment caveat 1)
verify: events.jsonl shows one ready->working->ready cycle on the UUID
SessionMetrics.ModelName after turn_end is non-empty and != 'unknown' (the normalized default model, e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-5')
SessionMetrics.ContextWindow after turn_end is > 0 (context_management.context_window when present, else the capacity table's per-model window)
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and >= 1 assistant line
